seoTitle: Sunshine North vs Sunshine West — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunshine North and Sunshine West. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunshine North vs Sunshine West: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunshine North and Sunshine West.
heading: Sunshine North vs Sunshine West — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunshine North, Victoria and Sunshine West, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunshine West has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Sunshine North (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunshine North or Sunshine West safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunshine North has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Sunshine West scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunshine North scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Sunshine West scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunshine North or Sunshine West?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunshine West has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
